verdict_reasoning: TMED9 encodes a p24 family transmembrane protein that cycles between the ER and Golgi as a cargo receptor/coat protein component. Its steady-state localization is ER/cis-Golgi membranes with the luminal domain facing the ER/Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. Checking each contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — no published evidence of stress- or transformation-induced PM display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific PM data; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — p24 proteins are COPI/COPII clients, not lysosomal TM proteins; (4) dual_localization — no surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, or surface proteomics data showing a PM pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — it is not secreted and no evidence of post-translational anchoring to a PM partner. No therapeutic programs targeting TMED9 on the cell surface are known. The NCBI summary explicitly describes ER/Golgi localization. Verdict: endomembrane resident.
